clinical.presenting_complaints: Patient presented with retrosternal chest discomfort on exertion (climbing 1-2 flights of stairs) for the past 6 weeks, associated with mild breathlessness, relieved within 5-10 minutes of rest. No discomfort at rest. No radiation to arm or jaw at presentation. No associated diaphoresis or palpitations.
clinical.initial_assessment: General Condition Conscious, cooperative, well-built, no pallor, icterus, cyanosis, clubbing, or pedal oedema. Weight / Height 74 kg / 169 cm | BMI: 25.9. Vitals BP 138/88 mmHg, Pulse 82/min, SpO2 98% (room air), Temp 98.4 F, RR 16/min. CVS S1 S2 heard, no murmurs, no added sounds, regular rhythm. Respiratory Air entry bilateral, clear. Abdomen Soft, non-tender, no organomegaly. CNS No focal neurological deficits.
clinical.significant_past_history: Chronic Illnesses Hypertension — 8 years, well controlled on Tab. Telmisartan 40mg OD. Previous Surgeries None. Current Medications Tab. Telmisartan 40mg OD; Tab. Aspirin 75mg OD; Tab. Atorvastatin 40mg OD. Known Allergies None. Smoking / Alcohol Ex-smoker (quit 5 years ago, 10 pack-years), Occasional alcohol. Family History Father had myocardial infarction at age 62.
clinical.primary_diagnosis_at_admission: Stable Angina, Single-Vessel Coronary Artery Disease (Proximal LAD, 80% stenosis)
clinical.final_diagnosis_at_discharge: Stable Angina, Single-Vessel Coronary Artery Disease (Proximal LAD, 80% stenosis)
clinical.key_investigations: Haemoglobin (Hb), Serum Creatinine, Lipid Profile (LDL), Blood Sugar Fasting, Troponin I, Resting ECG, Treadmill Test (TMT), Chest X-Ray, Coronary Angiography.
clinical.investigation_findings: Haemoglobin (Hb) 14.1 g/dL Normal. Serum Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL Normal. Lipid Profile (LDL) 142 mg/dL High. Blood Sugar Fasting 102 mg/dL Normal. Troponin I Negative Normal. Resting ECG Sinus rhythm, no acute ST-T changes Normal. Treadmill Test (TMT) Positive — ST depression at peak exercise, Bruce stage II Positive. Chest X-Ray Normal cardiac size, no active cardiopulmonary disease Normal. Coronary Angiography LAD 80% proximal stenosis, single-vessel disease, LVEF 58% Confirmed.
clinical.treatment_given: Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty (PTCA) — Proximal LAD. Technique Right radial artery access. Coronary angiography confirmed 80% stenosis in proximal LAD. Lesion crossed with guidewire, predilated with balloon. Single Bare Metal Stent deployed at the lesion site with good angiographic result, TIMI III flow restored. No intraoperative complications. Post-procedure stable, vitals monitored, access site clean and dry, no haematoma. Serial ECGs unremarkable. Cardiac enzymes post-procedure within normal limits. Ambulated same evening. Afebrile, asymptomatic, vitals stable, access site healing well. Declared medically fit for discharge.
clinical.operative_findings: Single-vessel proximal LAD disease confirmed. Successful stent deployment, no dissection, no perforation, no residual stenosis.
clinical.final_procedure_performed: Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty (PTCA) — Proximal LAD

DISCHARGE SUMMARY

SECTION 2 — ADMISSION & DISCHARGE DETAILS

Date & Time of Discharge 22/06/2026 at 10:30 hrs
Total Duration of Stay 2 Days
Type of Admission Planned / Elective
Ward / Room Category General Ward
Treating Consultant Dr. Senthil Kumaran
Registration Number TN-MED-33761
Specialty Cardiology
PMJAY Package Booked MC011A — PTCA, inclusive of diagnostic angiogram
Pre-Auth Reference PMJAY-PREAUTH-2026-TCNEW05-001
SECTION 3 — CLINICAL SUMMARY
Presenting Complaints:
Retrosternal chest discomfort on exertion for 6 weeks, relieved by rest. No discomfort at rest.
Relevant Medical History:
Hypertensive for 8 years, well controlled on Telmisartan 40mg OD. Non-diabetic. No previous cardiac events.
Pre-procedure Examination:
BP 138/88 mmHg, Pulse 82/min, SpO2 98%, afebrile. CVS: S1 S2 heard, no murmurs. All systemic examination within
normal limits.
SECTION 4 — DIAGNOSIS
Provisional Diagnosis (Admission) Stable Angina, Single-Vessel Coronary Artery Disease (Proximal LAD, 80% stenosis)
— ICD-10: I25.110
SECTION 5 — PROCEDURE PERFORMED
Procedure Name Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty (PTCA) — Proximal LAD
PMJAY Package MC011A — PTCA, inclusive of diagnostic angiogram
Date of Surgery 21/06/2026 at 09:30 hrs
Duration 40 minutes
Anaesthesia Local Anaesthesia with Conscious Sedation
Technique Right radial artery access. Coronary angiography confirmed 80% stenosis in proximal
LAD. Lesion crossed with guidewire, predilated with balloon. Single Bare Metal Stent
deployed at the lesion site with good angiographic result, TIMI III flow restored. No
intraoperative complications.
Intraoperative Finding Single-vessel proximal LAD disease confirmed. Successful stent deployment, no
dissection, no perforation, no residual stenosis.
Implant Used Coronary Stent for PDA stenting — Bare Metal. Brand: MedTronic, Model: BMS-3018,
Serial No: STN2026062101
SECTION 6 — POST-OPERATIVE COURSE
Day 1 (21/06/2026): Post-procedure stable, vitals monitored, access site clean and dry, no haematoma. Serial ECGs
unremarkable. Cardiac enzymes post-procedure within normal limits. Ambulated same evening.
Day 2 (22/06/2026): Afebrile, asymptomatic, vitals stable, access site healing well. Declared medically fit for discharge.
SECTION 7 — COMPLICATIONS
No intraoperative or post-procedure complications.
SECTION 9 — DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
# Medication Dose Frequency Duration
1 Tab. Aspirin 75mg 1 tablet Once daily Lifelong
2 Tab. Clopidogrel 75mg 1 tablet Once daily 12 Months
3 Tab. Atorvastatin 40mg 1 tablet Once daily (night) Lifelong
4 Tab. Telmisartan 40mg 1 tablet Once daily Continued
5 Tab. Metoprolol 25mg 1 tablet Twice daily Continued
SECTION 11 — DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH THIS DISCHARGE SUMMARY

was performed as documented, and all information in this discharge summary is true and correct to the best of my
